what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG line icon that depicts a speech bubble with a connected branch ending in a chevron-like node.
observations: Uses an inline SVG element with viewBox, width, height, fill, stroke, stroke-width, stroke-linecap, and stroke-linejoin attributes.
Draws the icon with two path elements.
Uses currentColor for stroke so the icon inherits text or parent color.
The first path forms a speech bubble with a tail.
The second path extends from the bubble area to the right and down into a branching chevron shape.
standouts: Combines chat and branching/tree imagery in one glyph.
conventions: Uses no fill and rounded stroke caps and joins.
Keeps styling on the root svg rather than per path.
Uses path-only geometry with no title, desc, ids, or classes.
concerns: No accessible title or description is present in the SVG itself.
notes: A reusable line icon for a branching chat or conversation-tree concept.
questions: Should this SVG be decorative only, or should it carry an accessible label when used?
Is the chevron intended to indicate a child node, expansion, or flow direction?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename chat-tree.svg matches the depicted chat bubble with branching connector.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a small, themeable icon representing threaded or branching chat interactions.
novelty: The icon merges a speech bubble with a partial tree/branch indicator rather than showing only a standard chat bubble.
contribution: Contributes a compact, currentColor SVG asset for representing conversation branching or chat-tree navigation in a fused design system.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 currentColor SVG line icon showing a speech bubble connected to a branching chevron-like node.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for representing threaded, branching, or tree-structured chat behavior as a reusable icon asset.
